senses.definition: a person who practices or teaches rol fing, a form of deep-tissue massage and movement therapy intended to relax and align the body
examples: The rolfer guided my posture during the session.
As a certified rolfer, she focuses on myofascial release.

senses.definition: plural of rolfer; multiple practitioners who perform rol fing
examples: The rolfers available in the city include several specialists.
Rolfers gathered for a regional workshop.

senses.definition: a system of bodywork and movement therapy aimed at improving posture and function; also called Somatic Relational Technique
examples: Rolfing is a form of deep tissue therapy.
She studied Rolfing to help clients with chronic back pain.

senses.definition: plural form of Rolfing sessions
examples: He has had several Rolfing sessions this year.
Different rolfers offer varying rolfing approaches.

senses.definition: plural of Rolfe or similar surname; may refer to persons named Rolfe
examples: The Rolfs attended the conference.
Mr. Rolfs is a renowned historian.

senses.definition: to move by turning over and over in a circular or curving path; to turn or rotate
examples: The ball began to roll down the hill.
She rolled the dough flat for the pastry.
Their eyes rolled in disbelief.

senses.definition: a cylindrical object or a roll of something; a list or lineup of people or things
examples: He paid for the roll at the bakery.
The roll of employees includes several new hires.

senses.definition: a roll of photographic film or a similar item
examples: She loaded a new roll into the camera.
The photographer used a roll of color film.

senses.definition: able to be rolled or turned; capable of rolling or being rolled
examples: The mattress is rollable for easy storage.
A rollable screen is convenient for travel.

senses.definition: a bed that can be rolled away and stored under another bed or furniture
examples: The guest room has a rollaway bed.
They stored the rollaway in the closet when not used.

senses.definition: plural of rollaway bed or similar furniture
examples: Rollaways are useful for hosting guests.
Two rollaways were pulled out for the sleepover.

senses.definition: a reversal of a decision or policy to a previous state
examples: The government announced a rollback of the tax increases.
They implemented a rollback on the new software feature.

senses.definition: to revert a change to a previous state
examples: The company decided to rollback the software update.
They rolled back the new rules after feedback.

senses.definition: plural of rollback; several reversals of decisions or changes
examples: There were multiple rollbacks during the rollout.
The rollbacks limited the new features temporarily.

senses.definition: a protective bar that rolls or a bar on a mechanical device
examples: The rollbar protects the window from damage.
The rollbar on the machine helps stabilize the load.

senses.definition: plural of rollbar; multiple protective bars
examples: The vehicle has several rollbars for safety.
Rollbars are standard in some off-road vehicles.

senses.definition: a collar that can be rolled down or up, often on a coat or shirt
examples: He preferred a rollcollar for extra warmth.
The rollcollar adds a casual touch to the sweater.

senses.definition: plural of rollcollar
examples: The jackets with rollcollars are popular in autumn.
Rollcollars provide a comfy neckline.

senses.definition: to move by turning over and over; to form or cover with a layer by rolling
examples: She rolled the dough into a thin sheet.
The carpet was rolled up for storage.

senses.definition: having been turned or moved by rolling; curved or bent
examples: A rolled towel lay on the bench.
Rolled oats are ready to cook.

senses.definition: a brand or term possibly referring to a roll-shaped edible item or product; unclear common usage
examples: They tasted a chip named rollejee at the fair.

senses.definition: a tool or device with a cylindrical shape used to roll things, or a person who rollers
examples: She used a paint roller to cover the wall.
The roller on the machine moved steadily.

senses.definition: a vehicle with a rolling drum or roller used in construction
examples: A road roller compacted the asphalt.
The roller moved slowly along the site.

senses.definition: a ballpoint pen using a rolling ball to deliver ink
examples: He signed the form with a rollerball pen.
Rollerball pens glide smoothly across paper.

senses.definition: plural of rollerball pen or similar items
examples: They distributed rollerballs to the conference attendees.
Rollerballs are convenient for quick notes.

senses.definition: a brand name used for inline skates; a type of skating equipment
examples: She bought new rollerblades for the rink.
Rollerblading is a fun outdoor activity.

senses.definition: to skate using inline skates; to move on wheels attached to the shoes
examples: He rollerbladed along the river path.
They rollerbladed for miles without stopping.

senses.definition: a person who rollerblades; a skater who uses inline skates
examples: The rollerblader performed a trick at the park.
Rollerbladers gather at the weekend event.

senses.definition: plural of rollerblader
examples: Rollerbladers from many cities joined the meetup.
The group of rollerbladers practiced daily.

senses.definition: the inline skates themselves; a brand-related term for inline skates
examples: She bought new rollerblades for skating.
Rollerblades help you glide smoothly.
